description: UE5 landscape: terrain creation, sculpting, painting layers, grass type, landscape materials, and landscape proxies

entries.title: Landscape System Overview
entries.description: The Landscape system creates large-scale outdoor terrain with sculpting, material painting, foliage, and optimized rendering. It supports Nanite (UE5.4+) and World Partition for massive worlds.
entries.whatItIs: ALandscapeProxy is UE5's terrain system that renders large outdoor environments using a heightmap-based mesh. It provides sculpting tools for shaping terrain, a layer-based painting system for applying multiple materials (grass, rock, dirt), and integration with foliage, NavMesh, and World Partition.
whenToUse: Outdoor environments with rolling hills, mountains, valleys, or plains
Large open-world terrain that needs efficient LOD and streaming
When you need to paint multiple surface types (grass, rock, sand, snow) with blending
When you need runtime grass spawning via Grass Type associated with landscape layers
whenNotToUse: Indoor environments or small rooms — use static meshes and BSP instead
Vertical surfaces like cave walls or cliffs (as primary geometry) — landscape is a heightmap, not volumetric
When you need destructible terrain at runtime — landscape doesn't support runtime modification of the heightmap in shipping builds easily
Extremely small playable areas — the minimum landscape resolution is overkill for small scenes
commonMistakes: Choosing the wrong landscape resolution — too many components = slow rendering, too few = blocky terrain. Start with recommended sizes (63x63 quads per component).
Not setting up landscape layers in the material before trying to paint — the Paint mode won't show layers without a proper Landscape Layer Blend node
Forgetting to create LayerInfo objects for each layer — required before painting a layer for the first time
Using tessellation instead of Nanite for landscape detail (UE5.4+) — Nanite landscape is more efficient
Not using World Partition with large landscapes — causes memory issues from loading the entire landscape at once
keyPoints: Create: Landscape Mode (Shift+2) > Manage > New Landscape. Set resolution and component count.
Sculpt: raise, lower, flatten, smooth, erosion tools for shaping the terrain heightmap
Paint: apply landscape layers (materials) using paint brushes. Each layer uses a LayerInfo object.
Landscape Material: use LandscapeLayerBlend node to blend between multiple material layers based on painted weights
Grass Type: ULandscapeGrassType spawns grass meshes procedurally based on layer paint density
Components: landscape is split into components for streaming. Each component has its own LOD.
Nanite support (UE5.4+): landscape can use Nanite for virtualized geometry LOD
Collision: landscape provides collision automatically. Use ECC_WorldStatic for traces.
